stories.title: The View from Patriots Wire
stories.summary:
Came across this article:
	
	“The Patriots have seemingly pulled away in the race with the many moves they've made in the offseason.



	 



	Meanwhile, the Bills haven't done much of anything to signal they'll be any better than they were last year. One could even argue that they'll take a step back after firing head coach Sean McDermott. Josh Allen is Superman on the football field, but he's going to need more than D.J. Moore and a middle-of-the-road defense to fly high enough to reach his first Super Bowl….



	 



	The Bills have to be seeing the moves being made by the Patriots, and it would be hard to imagine them feeling good about their offseason in relation to the competition. They are now faced with a situation where the Patriots have one of the best young quarterbacks in the league in Drake Maye and a top-10 defensive unit that gave Allen problems last season.



	 



	Will the Bills ever wake up and realize fighting fire with fire is much better than laying the hopes and dreams of an entire franchise on the shoulders of one man?”



	 



	Do you think this is justified or comically arrogant?

stories.title: Lions CB Terrion Arnold facing multiple felony charges - potential life in prison
Sabres sign Zach Benson to new seven-year contract
stories.summary:
Feisty 21-year-old forward locked up through 2032-33 on a $7.5 million/year AAV contract



The Buffalo Sabres have been very busy with reshaping the roster this summer moving out Michael Kesselring, Bowen Byram and Alex Tuch. However, the move they made in the last hour or so is likely the most popular of them all, extending winger Zach Benson with a new seven-year contract. 



The forward has been excellent value for the money especially in the just-finished season, ending up with 13 goals and 30 assists in the 65 regular season games playing up and down the lineup. Benson then added 5 goals and 4 assists in 13 playoff games as the Sabres finally ended their barren postseason streak and were within one goal of the Eastern Conference Finals. 




SEVEN MORE YEARS OF BENNY ⚔️We have signed Zach Benson to a seven-year contract with an AAV of $7.5 million.Details → https://t.co/GWcREJzmxC pic.twitter.com/7OHpLtjZeN— Buffalo Sabres (@BuffaloSabres) June 25, 2026




Benson’s brand of grit and passion saw him quickly become a fan favorite especially so in his third season, and he was set to become a restricted free agent in a week when free agency opens. 



General Manager Jarmo Kekäläinen acknowledged the value of the feisty forward – 



“I see him as a true core piece that fills all the boxes. I can’t say enough good things about Zach Benson. He’s got skill, he’s got hockey sense, he’s got instincts, he’s relentless, he’s a competitor. What a great playoff he had and, again, I’m so excited about the level that he can get to, still. … Just wait until he becomes the big, strong man that we’re going to be pushing him to get to.”




Zach Benson, extended 7 x $7.5M by BUF, is a young two-way playmaking winger who compensates for his lack of size and speed through brains, skill, and the unshakeable desire to be an absolute pain in the ass to everyone he plays against. Just needs to start scoring. pic.twitter.com/Qo8hLUQ89R— JFresh (@JFreshHockey) June 25, 2026

stories.title: Sign and Trade Complete, Tuch to the Capitals
stories.summary:
Jarmo Salvages a 3rd Rounder Instead of Alex Walking Away for Nothing



Alex Tuch is officially not a Buffalo Sabre. The main piece in the Jack Eichel trade is now a Washington Capital, with enough money to line the Reflecting Pool in D.C. Sabres’ General Manager Jarmo Kekalainen signed Tuch to a 10.5 million, 8 year deal, a contract too rich for Buffalo’s cap crunching issues and shipped him off to Washington. Buffalo will receive a third-round draft pick for Tuch, who was scheduled to become an unrestricted free agent on July 1.




The Sabres will get a third-round pick from the Capitals as part of the sign-and-trade with Alex Tuch. Tuch will get 8 x $10.5M on his new team.— Pierre LeBrun (@PierreVLeBrun) June 24, 2026




Tuch scored 309 points (139 goals and 170 assists) in 360 games with Buffalo. He was a fan favorite for his short handed goals and strong penalty killing. The big right winger was vital to the Sabres’ turnaround last season and his loss will have Jarmo searching for some top-six offense. “He’s going to be missed, but we’ll be fine,” a confident Kekalainen said in his press conference on Wednesday.



That 3rd round pick (likely in 2027) will supply Buffalo with either more future draft capital or more ammunition for wheeling and dealing. It is certainly better that letting number 89 walk out the door with nothing to show for it. It became clear that the cost and term to retain his services was too much for the team. The gap was too wide on both counts and Tuch was giving out no “hometown discounts”. It’s the nature of the sports world we live in. It still leaves a sour taste in the mouths of the fans, though. 



It seems the desire to move out west for his west coast wife was not part of the equation after all, just the money and term. He made out on that front. I know most will wish him well in his career and thank him for his service. He was a great representative of what it meant to be a hockey player in Buffalo. He was Mr. Buffalo Sabre for five big years. Despite that, he moves on while dissappointing the greater fanbase and reminding us all that a little loyalty is still hard to come by these days. You would think after the AMAZING turnaround this past season, he would have wanted to remain a part of that, somehow, someway.

stories.title: If There Was a First Song to be Played at the New Stadium What Song Would You Like it to Be ?
stories.summary:
So the Bills will be opening their new home Highmark Stadium this season & I was wondering what song would you all think should be fitting for the very first jam on their brand new Kick A** sound system with the Mafia on hand ? 



	 



	Of course one of the all time favorite sayings that is bound to be used is the Marvism "Where would you rather be than right here - Right now" So I thought if they could incorporate this song with that Marvism it would be fitting .



	 



	I know we have had other posts in the past of songs that we felt should be played when the team comes on the field & such but this one I would like to keep just a bit different ! Lets keep this one to something to do about a new start, a new home, Starting over, something of that sort . But it's got to rock, no slushy love song type songs  this is the Bills new home so lets start it by blowing the top of the place .



	 



	I love the intro to this song which could be playing in the back ground with the stadium lights doing some cool type of visual thing to match the piano riff while someone was doing a introduction of sorts like "where else would you rather be than Right here (then BANG the lyrics & fans come in) - RIGHT NOW !!! Jam on !!



	 



	Then of course at the end fire works  all around the canopy !! 



	 



	So give out some ideas as far as the song but remember keep it on the subject of something new as I said in the previous sentence ... 



	 



	What say you Mafia ???

stories.title: 2026 World Cup, Matchday 14: Match thread and discussion
Sabres Unload Byram and Greenway to Chicago For a Nice Haul
stories.summary:
Buffalo Cashes in With the 4th Overall pick, the 45th pick and Dman Louis Crevier



Buffalo Sabres general manager Jarmo Kekäläinen cleared out some major league cap space for the team by moving starry-eyed Bowen Byram and 4th liner Jordan Greenway. Bo and his agent have become a headache for the Blue and Gold with his desire to become a number one defender with the salary to match. Greenway’s earnings just didn’t match the production, even though he had some nice attributes.



The Sabres are now in possession of the 4th overall pick in this week’s draft. They could grab a top talent in that range who could be a real difference maker soon. Or they might use it as ammunition along with other assets to nab a legit star to add to this roster. Jarmo is about the here and now mostly, so I tend to believe that the pick could be on the move.




Louis Crevier, acquired by BUF, is a defenceman who is 6'8 every time he steps on the ice and likes to shoot the puck. The Hawks played him in really tough matchup minutes last season, and he sure did hit people a lot in those minutes. pic.twitter.com/GZ8BsaJNq4— JFresh (@JFreshHockey) June 24, 2026




Buffalo also has a 2nd round pick again, getting Chicago’s 45th overall. Some good talent should be available there. Also coming over in the deal is hulking giant defensemen Louis Crevier. The 25 year old is 6’8″ and nearly 230 pounds. He is a decent skater for his size and scored a surprising 7 goals along with 25 points for the Blackhawks last year, his first full season in the league. He is not a Bo replacement but should challenge for a bottom pair spot. 



Mar 17, 2026; Chicago, Illinois, USA;  Chicago Blackhawks defenseman Louis Crevier (46) celebrates with center Frank Nazar (91) after scoring a goal against the Minnesota Wild during the first period at United Center. Mandatory Credit: Matt Marton-Imagn Images



GMJK now has the room and money to really cook. Benson should be signed soon and so could Alex Tuch now. They could make it work. Or Buffalo may sign and trade him for options elsewhere. More to come soon from the Sabres for sure, but a nice job by Jarmo on this one.

stories.title: Defense giving up big plays vs 12 play drives
stories.summary:
This topic drives me crazy. 
	 



	Heard it addressed on Locked On this morning.



	 



	”Well, you wanted a defense that gets after it, but with that comes giving up big plays.. McDermott’s defense didn’t do that.”  Summarizing a point made by Jerry O.  And he’s not wrong, but… 



	 



	Ok, cool… What his Defense did was allow teams to bleed clock, wear down our defense, convert third downs and then eventually score.   Thus, shortening games and putting more pressure on Josh Allen to score when he has the ball.  All the while operating in a run-heavy structure to protect the defense, that bleeds the clock, limits his pass attempts and makes him press to do something with it when he drops back.  
	 



	I understand the analytics of it.. and the fact is, drives that have an explosive play in them are far more likely to end in a score than those without them.   Noted. 
	 



	However, with the new kickoff rules and teams starting so much closer to midfield, I will take a defense that gives up 2-3 more big plays per game, as long as they are creating 6-8+ big plays themselves… turnovers, sacks, tfl’s etc. 



	 



	Case in point.  Denver playoff game.  Josh Allen spazzing before half.  Why did he do that?   It’s simple.. he saw an entire playoff half go by where he was almost perfect, but because of a James Cook fumble and the Defense not getting off the field - therefore limiting his drive chances - he was heading into halftime down.   So he pressed, and made it worse.  McDermott’s defensive philosophy affecting Josh Allen in a nutshell. 
	 



	The 3rd down stops and big plays for our Defense have to be there, but as long as they are.. everyone downvote me the first time I whine about a 65 yard explosive given up by our Defense in a Gamethread.  
	 



	Bend, don’t break only works when you’re actually bending and not breaking and not affecting your own QB while his counterpart elite QB’s don’t just break your defense when it matters.. they massacre it.

stories.title: Jammerz One & Only Off-Season GMFAD
stories.summary:
Before I start just wanted to say there will be some important and tough decisions to be made. Mainly cap decisions. Big one is letting Alex Tuch go. It’s for the best. With our emerging young forwards it just doesn’t make any real sense to tie up around $10-$11 mill in one forward who is good for what 60-66 points? Even during the season if he didn’t get hot late he would’ve ended with 20 some goals and 50 some points. That much cap in a guy who will be 30 before the season starts and is already trending in the wrong direction isn’t what this franchise needs. This is literally a setup for another Jeff Skinner type contract debacle, to which we are still suffering with. 



Also Bowen Byram and his agent are at it again so yup he’s gone. In my perfect off-season I would love to re-sign Byram to a $9 mill contract but that’s not what he wants. He wants to be the de facto  #1D. He won’t get that here. He’s a must trade this offseason. Waiting and waiting is just going to be him waving goodbye without anything in return. 



Some. young players are ready to take the next step while some need a new type of guidance IMHO so some of the overpriced guys will be traded in my perfect offseason. 



The moves I’m making are mainly for both now and the future. My main philosophy is to be more defensive, get more and more and more physical and bring in a #1 centre. This will be a team made for the playoffs. Oh yes Helenius is well and ready to take over our #2 centre duties. 



My main goals for the offseason is get rid of the players that don’t want to be here, keep an eye out on our future, add high energy go go players, add a #1 centre, add leadership, add very physical players. 



Coaching & Management 



Fire Marty Wilford 



Need a full Lindy coaching staff. Someone who he’s trusted before. 



Replacement – James Patrick 



James comes into take over for Lindy after his 2 seasons but until then he will be the second in command. This guy is huge for young player development and he knows the ropes already. This is more of a 2 years down the road hiring so nobody else nabs him like the Isles did with McKee. 



Fire Seth Appert 



If Appert even remotely put together a good PP our team’s offense would be top 2-3 in the NHL. 



Replacement (one of) – Jessica Campbell or Vinny Prospal. 



Kraken did not utilize Jessica Campbell at all. Her specialty was the PP but the Kraken mainly just used her in a skating coach and skills development way. I think she’d be a great coach for our PP. 



I had Vinny Prospal as our next PP coach but he was sniped by the Blues. 



Fire Mark Jakubowski



Mark has been here since the Tim Murray days. He’s started here as AGM then to VP of Hockey Ops then back to AGM. Being part of that drought means he was part of some awful teams that weren’t ran all that well and high majority of the picks turned out to be nothing. 



Replacement – Shane Doan 



Let’s make this a family affair. Outside of the obvious he does bring a lot to the table. Is looking for a bigger role inside an organization and I think this would be the right fit for him. It’d be nice to see someone like Shane Doan join the organization in the front office. 



Re-Signings



Zach Benson 7 years @ $6.95 mill per



Notes



Exact same deal as Doaner. They’re worth pretty much the same. Obviously getting back a huge player for the long haul. 



Beck Malenstyn  5 years @ $3.3 mill per



Notes



This is a no brainer. He is our 4th line. This is the only UFA I’m re-signing. Yes that means bye to Tuch. Trust me you’ll thank me later. 



Trades



1st Trade 



To St. Louis Blues



Alex Tuch’s Rights



Josh Norris 



Bo Byram



Brodie Ziemer



20th Overall



To Buffalo Sabres



Robert Thomas 



Jordan Kyrou



Torey Krug



11th Overall



Notes 



We finally land our #1 centre. He adds a lot. Great on the PP. Great on the face-off dot. Most importantly the guy can play more than 40 games a season. Norris is just too injury prone to be an effective player for this team. He was also very underwhelming in the playoffs. 



We get a good offensive winger in Kyrou and we move up to the 11th overall pick. Kyrou can also play every forward position and is good defensively, is fast in transition and fits great with the current team. Kyrou can switch to centre if Helenius does indeed struggle as 2C (but I don’t see that happening.)



We also take on Krug’s last season as a cash (notice I said cash not cap) help for the Blues. He’s LTIR so we are just paying him. 



2nd Trade 



To Winnipeg Jets



Ukko-Pekka Luukkonen



Devon Levi



Jack Quinn



Peyton Krebs



Jordan Greenway 



2027 1st Round Pick



To Buffalo Sabres



Connor Hellebuyck



Notes 



Here we acquire one of the best goalies in the league. Jets have no future or present in goaltending if they trade Hellebuyck so in this deal they go with a UPL/Levi tandem. UPL being their short term answer and Levi being their long term answer. This is the right time to trade UPL. There’s a good chance his numbers stumble yet again after a solid yet short campaign. 



3rd Trade



To Edmonton Oilers



Alex Lyon 



To Buffalo Sabres



2026 2nd Round Draft Pick



Notes



We honestly have to get rid of this 3 goalie thing. Edmonton is desperate. Strike while the iron is hot with Lyon.  



4th Trade



To San Jose Sharks



Jason Zucker



Vsevolod Komarov



2026 2nd Round Draft Pick (Edmonton)



To Buffalo Sabres



Joshua Ravensbergen 



Notes



Hated doing this one as Jason is a great guy to have in the locker room but he just doesn’t fit anymore and I don’t want him playing what’s left of his career in the press box. So I thought of the best possible place for him that’s decently close to his home. San Jose add a vet that adds around 45+ points. 



In this trade we add our future number 1 goalie in Joshua Ravensbergen. This kid’s the real deal folks. San Jose has Askarov thus that makes Ravensbergen expendable. Once Hellebuyck’s contract is through he and Ellis should be ready to go and compete as a tandem. 



Draft



I’m only doing the first pick for us in this GMFAD. So without further ado the hometown Buffalo Sabres are proud to select from the United States Development Program LW/RW Wyatt Cullen, son of former NHLer Matt Cullen. 



Scouting Report 




Tremendous skater and has incredible speed



Very elusive player 



His vision is one of the tops in the class



High hockeyIQ



Great at both playmaking and scoring



He lives to drive offensive play



Great hands



Would be a great PP guy at the NHL level if developed the right way



That’s all about his offensive game, his defensive game well let’s just say it’s mainly a work in progress. 




Free Agency 



Sign Jacob Trouba to 4 years @ $6.5 mill per season. 



Notes



We replace Byram with Trouba. Here we add a very good two way defenseman. Great on the PK, great defensive game, great shot blocking abilities, can contribute offensively when needed and he is a beast that just loves to kick ass. We lose bit on the offensive side of things with Byram out and Trouba being his replacement but the gain in physicality, defensiveness and leadership makes it all worth it. 



Sign AJ Greer to 5 years @ $3.5 mill per season. 



Notes 



We add another unreal bottom 6 player who hits, has a high intensity forecheck, kills penalties, and a motor that just won’t stop. Think Malenstyn but for our LW. He can fill in on the 3rd line if an injury occurs.



Sign Jake Bean to 1 year @ $1.1 mill per season 



Notes



Just a depth signing. Mobile defenseman that can play the PP if injuries occur. 



Lineup



Forwards



Benson – Thomas – Thompson



Kyrou – Helenius – Doan



Kulich – McLeod – Ostlund



Greer – Carrick – Malenstyn 



Danforth/Kozak



Defense



Samuelsson – Dahlin



Power – Trouba



Metsa – Timmins



Johnson/Bean



Goalies



Hellebuyck 



Ellis



Note



I’m very sorry if this is somewhat messed up with spacing and word size and everything. I do not have a computer right now but hopefully you guys enjoyed the read. I know unrealistic but a man can wish.

stories.title: Surfeit of goalies means Sabres could trade Devon Levi this summer
stories.summary:
BUFFALO – On Jan. 20, 2025, following two months in the minors, goalie Devon Levi made a rare start for the Sabres, stopping 28 shots in a 6-4 road loss to the Kraken.
A day later, they sent Levi back to the AHL, where he spent the next season and a half with the Rochester Americans.
No one knew it that day in Seattle, but Levi, once a prospect so highly regarded that many viewed ... Read the full article 
The post Surfeit of goalies means Sabres could trade Devon Levi this summer first appeared on Buffalo Hockey Beat.

stories.title: Buffalo Sabres Open Thread, Wk of 6/22
stories.summary:
It’s been a dramatic last few days with some big trades getting made, while rumors continue to fly around the futures of certain Buffalo Sabres players including Alex Tuch and Bowen Byram. Defenseman Michael Kesselring has been shipped out and word is that forward Zach Benson is going to receive a big payday too.



This is NHL Draft week as well, being held in Buffalo from June 26 to June 27, 2026. The Sabres currently have four picks: Round 1 (20th overall), Round 4, Round 5 & Round 6.

stories.title: Hurricanes fans jam downtown Raleigh for Stanley Cup parade
stories.summary: Thousands of Hurricanes fans crammed onto sidewalks, gathered around office building windows, even lined up on floor after floor of at least one parking deck to cheer and wave at the team's Stanley Cup championship parade Saturday.

stories.title: Josh and Hailee’s daughter’s name has been revealed
stories.summary: Go Bills!

stories.title: Road to the Draft, Rumors, Trades and Signings
stories.summary:
The Sabres Need to Jump on Things NOW



The trade of Michael Kesselring wasn’t unexpected but it was disappointing to move off this player so quickly. Injuries derailed his season and he never secured favorability with head coach Lindy Ruff. The Sabres essentially dumped his salary to move up 7 spots in the draft, probably for better trade capital. Hopefully GMJK makes a big splash on draft weekend because there is a real chance Buffalo will have no picks in the first three rounds if that top pick is on the move.



We all know the Alex Tuch situation. It appears highly probable he has played his final games as a Buffalo Sabre. If I’m Jarmo, a sign and trade attempt should be in the works. Don’t let him walk for nothing, make every attempt to collect some sort of asset for him. A second round pick, a bottom six defenseman, get something. If this reality isn’t enough of a downer, word on the street is that Bo Byram is not willing to extend with Buffalo. He has his agent in his ear and big money dollar signs swirling around his head. The Sabres can’t possibly take the Tuch approach again, HOPING he will come around this upcoming season. They must be proactive and use Byram as their top trade piece. 









At this point, I feel the Sabres should jump on the signings of their own players, and pronto. Don’t leave anything to chance. Leave Tuch and Byram in the rearview mirror at this point. (at least for now) Zach Benson is the new priority and Beck Malenstyn is essential as well. I would love to get Peyton Krebs signed too for a reasonable cost but he could be a trade asset now. I hope not. Take care of in-house business in the next week while still on the phone looking for deals as well. Get the rest of your own players secured then go from there. They can circle back to Tuch and Byram.



Players of interest for me include big winger Matthew Knies and his cost controlled contract. He is in year two of a six year contract for $7.75 million per season. He could easily slide into the top six, if not the top line. Defender Zach Whitecloud could play in the 4, 5 or 6 spot with Byram gone. He has loads of experiencee and is signed for two more years at a very reasonable $2.75 million per year. Robert Thomas remains the big elusive fish and the cost to acquire him would be the most expensive. That is, if the Blues are still listening.

stories.title: Bills sign RB Ian Wheeler
stories.summary: Wheeler is fresh off a United Football League championship season with the Louisville Kings where he was named the United Bowl MVP

stories.title: Sabres trade Kesselring to Sharks to move up seven spots in 2026 Draft
stories.summary:
The Buffalo Sabres have set the ball rolling on what could be a momentous summer for the franchise by trading away defenseman Michael Kesselring and their 27th-overall pick in the upcoming NHL Draft to San Jose Sharks, and getting back the 20th-overall pick. 



The Sharks had obtained that pick from the Edmonton Oilers for defenseman Jake Walman in March 2025. 



Kesselring only featured in 34 games with the Sabres with injuries limiting his playing time and utility for Buffalo. He was arguably the centerpiece of the trade that brought him and Josh Doan to Western New York from the Utah Mammoth, but was overshadowed in impact by the son of star defenseman forward Shane Doan. He had two assists and averaged 13:24 of ice time.



The defenseman was at the end of his two-year extension that he had signed with Utah and was set to be an RFA this summer. 



The NHL Draft will be held at KeyBank Center on Friday, June 26 and Saturday, June 27, and the Sabres have four picks: Round 1 (20th overall), Round 4, Round 5 & Round 6.

stories.title: State of the Sabres, Checklisting Areas of Concern
stories.summary:
Playoffs Over, Draft Fast Approaching, Jarmo Will Be Busy and Tested



Today we examine seven points of interest for Sabres fans as business will ramp up for the team and the NHL over the next several weeks. Buffalo has areas of concern they must handle correctly. Opinions in this article are my own, it would be a boring world if we all thought as one.



1. Alex Tuch



Mr. Buffalo Sabre himself is the big topic of the offseason for Buffalo. At this point, it feels like he will most assuredly test the free agent market. Some still feel like a last minute deal could be hammered out with the Sabres. No matter what happens, color me disappointed that one of the biggest faces of the franchise is going this route. I know it is his right and by NHL standards he is looking to maximize his earnings and it’s his career path and all that jazz. I’m just not seeing any compromise from Tuch’s camp. Pressing for more money when there is life-changing millions on the table already just never sits well with me. The Sabres presented a fair offer I feel, and they are surely willing to inch it up a little bit more to keep him. Tuch and his upstart teammates are in a good spot with Buffalo now, ending the curse and putting the kibosh on the dreadful playoff drought at long last. The future looks bright and Alex could be a well paid part of it, but his exit could really set the team back. There should be some concessions on both sides to get a deal done. For an average Joe struggling in today’s world, with skyrocketing expenses, it’s difficult to sympathize with millionaire negotiations. Sorry, that’s just me and my perspective. By all accounts, Buffalo is prepared to double Tuch’s salary at 9 to 9.5 million and offer him 7 years of term at least. That doesn’t seem good enough to Alex and his agent right now. If his family/living status is a part of this decision, well, there is not much Buffalo can do besides overpay wildly to keep him. That might not even do it. Growing up in nearby Syracuse and being a fan of the team as a child does not seem like a factor any more. The money and the term is paramount, along with his family. (with his west-coast wife being a possible influence)



Please don’t get me wrong, I love having Alex Tuch on the team and I hope they can sign him. It’s the staggering amount of money involved to retain our player and his apparent stubberness to not bend just a little that rubs me the wrong way. It’s not just Tuch for the record, he is just the current big name player in this position. Buffalo is a blue-collar town that just emerged from 14 years of misery with a division championship. You would think Mr. Sabre himself would want to remain a part of the resurgence and figure this out for the good of both sides.



General manager Jarmo Kekäläinen has his work cut out for him. How the Tuch situation plays out will directly effect Buffalo’s other moves. That will lay the groundwork for the Sabres’ off season. With Jeff Skinner’s biggest buyout hiccup coming in 2026-27, managing next year while fitting Tuch in is Jarmo’s biggest hurtle. If he can work it, Tuch’s new contract won’t be as much of a hindrance after that. Especially with the NHL’s salary cap going up more and more. Next season is the biggest and toughest obstacle for the new Buffalo GM.



In today’s NHL climate, teams must generally overpay for free agents, including their own. Not many players do the hometown discount anymore, even in ideal conditions. If the Sabres retain Tuch’s services, the likelyhood of him earning that money probably tops off at 5 more years. That is something Buffalo will have to deal with down the road. For now, what gives you the best chance to continue to win? Alex Tuch, most assuredly. But paying and keeping one of your own top players means enduring the loss of others teammates to make it happen. The BIG MONEY/sometimes overpaid stars of the league don’t seem to realize this or seem to care. Your team will take a hit to the talent on the roster in order to pay you mega-bucks to make sure you are happy, so I hope you are happy. Or is letting Tuch go at this point and exploring other options through trade or in-house promotions the move to make? That money saved can be used elsewhere if Jarmo can swing a deal. Buffalo had an eye out for a number one center upgrade already, adding a suitable replacement for number 89 as well will be a monumental task.









2. Benny, Beck, Byram and Krebs



These four players all require attention too, with Benson, Malenstyn and Krebs needing new contracts and Bowen Byram needing a contract extention. Any deal with Bo will thankfully not impact this year’s salary cap. Hopefully Double B liked the progression of the team and his role among the other players and is willing to extend. Otherwise, he is likely a main trade piece to aquire other top talent.



Meanwhile, Zach Benson is a must to sign. Buffalo would be wise to lock him up in a long term deal of at least six years. I originally had Taz signing a deal a little north of Josh Doan’s recent new contract. I think now it will be even more north than that after his playoffs. Even with the added expense, Benny is worth it. I like to imagine the second half of his deal being a bargain for the results he will put forth in four to six years.



Beck Malenstyn is a must signing to me as well. Something in the neighborhood of 3 years and 3.25 million per for Buffalo’s stud 4th liner. The guy is a workhorse and a great shot blocker. He is also physical and lays the body and it’s his efforts that make the 4th line go, even with interchangeable parts. I very much hope to retain Beck and his services. Buffalo has some solid bottom line pieces as cheap labor to offset his contract in Tyson Kozak, Justin Danforth and Sam Carrick.



Peyton Krebs is always a polarizing player around here but I love the guy. He enjoys being a Buffalo Sabre, he has a great work ethic and is an underrated leader for this team. I would like to see him in a third line role, as we saw some solid offensive production this past year. He has a strong two way game and is a key piece of the puzzle to me. Where his contract negotiations go, I don’t really know. He has been a solid, inexpensive worker-bee these past four years and deserves a raise. Hopefully that bump up in pay is still affordable for the team.



Mar 22, 2026; Anaheim, California, USA; Buffalo Sabres left wing Beck Malenstyn (29) controls the puck against the Ahaheim Ducks during the third period at Honda Center. Mandatory Credit: Gary A. Vasquez-Imagn Images



3. Sacrifices



If Tuch and company are to be signed, some of the higher priced, expendable players will need to be moved. Jason Zucker and Jordan Greenway are two top candidates, unfortunately. Between them, that is almost 9 million in cap space that could be cleared out for Tuch in particular. Both would be a jolting loss for the team, though not irreplaceable. Zucker may have been one of former GM Kevyn Adams’ best ever signing. The veteran winger scored 45 goals and nearly 100 points in two seasons with the Blue and Gold. He is also a power play specialist on a team with a much maligned man advantage. Zucker would surely be missed but Buffalo has young replacement players in house to fill his skates.



Jordan Greenway could also be on his way out if the Sabres can find somebody to manage his contract. The big man is a strong penalty killer, sound defensively and is Buffalo’s only real legitimate fighter. I’m sure the Sabres would love to keep him, at a more reasonable 2.5 million a year and not the hefty 4 million he currently makes. Alex Tuch’s stance and the RFAs will make that near impossible. 



Another potential casualty could be center Ryan McLeod and his 5 million per season deal. I don’t even want to discuss this option because McLeod to me is a vital part of the team. Elite quickness in his skating, solid two-way play and over 100 points for Buffalo in Ryan’s first two seasons. The Sabres paid up for him too with a strong prospect in Matthew Savoie sent back to Edmonton in return. I don’t want to see McLeod dispatched so quickly. I believe he still has a part to play in the Sabres’ resurgence.



Nov 5, 2024; Buffalo, New York, USA;  Ottawa Senators left wing Brady Tkachuk (7) and Buffalo Sabres left wing Jordan Greenway (12) fight during the first period at KeyBank Center. Mandatory Credit: Timothy T. Ludwig-Imagn Images



4. The Draft (First round, Friday, June 26th)



The live event is to be held in Buffalo again (first time since 2016) but the hometown team might not even have a selection in round one. Or round two either. (already traded to Ottawa) It all depends on Jarmo Kekäläinen’s wheelings and dealings. If the Sabres press hard for a number one center which has been speculated, Buffalo’s first overall selection in the draft will surely be in play. It is safe to say the team is in WIN NOW mode so Jarmo’s motives will no doubt reflect that. The GM nearly dealt 2025 first rounder Radim Mrtka already and could use him as trade bait again.



Buffalo’s big splash on draft day may well be a big trade for an experienced here and now player. I would estimate there is better than a 50-50 chance of that happening. But the deal might not be there, or the cost too great. Perhaps a move is made before or after the draft, anything is possible. While Buffalo’s 1st rounder may be up for grabs, there is an interesting variety of players that could be available if they keep the pick.



Brooks Rogowski, C, Oshawa (OHL) The hulking forward is Tage Thompson sized at 6’7″ but uses his big frame more to his advantage than TNT does. He is tough to defend, especially around the net and rarely loses puck battles. Rogowski had 42 points (15 goals, 27 assists) in 46 games this season and is committed to play at Michigan State this Fall.



Jack Hextall, C, Youngstown (USHL) The right-handed shot, high-end forward had 58 points (20 goals, 38 assists) in 59 games this season. Hextall is more of a playmaker, but he did find the net more often as the season went on. He has a strong two-way game already which should serve him well in the pros. The 6 foot, 188 pounder is also committed to Michigan State next season.



Maddox Dagenais, C, Quebec (QMJHL) If this talented and sometimes physical player is still avalable, Buffalo should highly consider him. He will almost assuredly be snatched up by Montreal with the next selection if the Sabres should pass. In 62 games, the 18 year old had 62 points (30 goals, 32 assists) with an impressive 25 power play points. Dagenais projects to be a solid number 2 center in the NHL.



Gleb Pugachyov, RW, Russia (KHL) This 6’3″ 200 pound Russian winger strikes fear into his opponents, literally. They are highly aware when he is on the ice and must keep their heads on a swivel. This power forward has a physical dominance not often seen these days as heavy contact is a big part of his game. He is not without skill, but he is more of a crash the net type of player. (never a bad thing) He is projected as a late first, early second round selection that Buffalo should have on their radar. Pugachyov is one of those players every other team craves and I really feel like the Sabres should take a closer look here.









5. Trading Places



A number one center and/or a possible Tuch replacement are the biggest upgrades/needs for the Sabres that will require dealing outside of the organization. Robert Thomas of the Blues seems to still be a favorite potential acquisition for Buffalo fans. St. Louis seemingly put a stop to the Thomas rumor mill, stating he would still be on the Blues’ roster in the Fall. They are NOT actively shopping him. Frankly, we have heard things like that before from teams with available players. Doing that relaxes one of your own and drives up the potential price tag, in theory. I feel St. Louis is still listening and the right offer could land the first round pick from 2017. Thomas turns 27 next month and has loads of experience and success. Through 530 NHL games, the Blues center has scored 460 points. His career high for goals is only 26 but he has notched 60 assists in a season twice.



Also in St. Louis, right winger Jordan Kyrou remains a possibility as a Tuch replacement, should he depart. Kyrou has scored 70 plus points 3 of the past 5 years and is a pretty consistent offensive threat. He doesn’t have Tuch’s defense and penalty killing ability though, so his overall game in not on the same level. Kyrou is a year older than Thomas and more likely to be moved.



Elsewhere, center Mason McTavish of the Ducks could be an option. McTavish had a falling off this season in points and with the team in general. He is a talented 23 year old, with 181 points through 304 NHL games. He had a career high 52 points the previous season (22g, 30a) but dropped off to 42 this past year. He still has room to grow and might benefit from a new enviroment. His defensive game also needs some improvement.



Another recent idea involves New Jersey stud Nico Hischier. The 27 year old center has been a leader for the Devils for years now. He has good history with Lindy Ruff, who named him team captain. Hischier has nearly 500 points through just over 600 NHL games. He is signed for one more year with a cap hit of $7,250,000. That could work for next season, with some salary going back New Jersey’s way in any transaction. If the Sabres were to acquire Hischier though, it would need to be a sign and trade acquisition, with him agreeing to a new contract with Buffalo. A substantial one. Any new Nico Hischier deal will be north of 11 million per season. Maybe even 12 or more. The Sabres would have a full year to make it work as that contract won’t kick in until 2027-28. They would avoid Jeff Skinnner’s biggest year of payout too. Hischier could also be a good mentor/influence on our young up and coming European players as well.



There is also a chance Jarmo goes off the board and is after a player nobody has in mind. That might actually be wise, as he would have little to no competition in bargaining. If he shocks us like that, we all hope it is a good shocking.



Mar 21, 2026; Vancouver, British Columbia, CAN;  St. Louis Blues center Robert Thomas (18) skates with the puck against Vancouver Canucks left wing Liam Ohgren (92) during the third period at Rogers Arena. Mandatory Credit: Simon Fearn-Imagn Images



6. The Young Core



The trio of Jiri Kulich, Noah Ostlund and Konsta Helenius all have a good chance of being in Buffalo’s top nine in the Fall of 2026. As far as I’m concernned, Ostlund has already graduated to the big club. In 60 games last season, he registered 27 points and was a +11. Noah looked the part with elite playmaking abilities and shifty stickhandling. I can see him scoring 50 points this year. The Sabres really missed him in the playoffs after he fell with injury just three games in against Boston. Ostlund should be right back on the team this season from my perspective. Kulich graduated too in my eyes, playing 62 games in 2024-25 (with 24 points) and starting last season in Buffalo as well before being pulled out of the lineup with his blood clot issues. By all accounts, he underwent a successful procedure at the Mayo Clinic in Rochester, Minnesota and is doing well now. He anticipates making a full return to the lineup for the upcoming season. As some have suggested, Jiri might spend some time with the Amerks to really get back in professional shape again. That is possible. But he has summer training and Sabres’ training camp to show he is ready to go. I think he will be.



Now, onto Konsta Helenius. While Kulich and Ostlund seem to have spots waiting for them, Konsta may have forced his way in with his strong post season cameo. (and his play in Rochester) That would make three talented wiz kids on the roster. By appearing in the playoffs for Buffalo, (and providing a needed spark) his entry-level contract (ELC) year was officially burned. As a result, the clock on his contract advanced, meaning he will reach restricted free agency one year earlier than if he had stayed in the minors. (or just didn’t play) So as far as I’m concerned, that means his time is now. Ostlund and Kulich have the better NHL experience but Helenius has the highest ceiling in my opinion. As we saw against Montreal in the post season, he looks NHL ready. Having all three in the lineup not only injects youthful talent, it also helps Buffalo save on cap space. That will be huge, depending on what happens with Tuch and the RFA’s. Plus any big trade that might go down, bringing in a high salary player. Zucker, Greenway, maybe even Jack Quinn might be involved in Jarmo’s dealings. The wiz kids provide adequate replacement level talent on the cheap, for this season anyway.



Of course, GMJK could also involve one of the youngins’ I have just discussed here in a trade too. I sure hope not, I have high hopes for all three. Helenius should be off the table for sure, Jarmo!



Jan 27, 2026; Toronto, Ontario, CAN; Buffalo Sabres forward Noah Ostlund (86) controls the puck against Toronto Maple Leafs defenseman Troy Stetcher (28) during the second period at Scotiabank Arena. Mandatory Credit: John E. Sokolowski-Imagn Images



7. Free Agency



Lastly, we come to free agency. One of the best players available is one of Buffalo’s own. And by recent reports, the Sabres and Tuch’s camp are still far apart. Unless there is a last minute miracle or Buffalo is able to pull off a sign and trade deal for some value back, Alex could depart with the Sabres having nothing to show for it. The 2026 free agency class is widely considered to be one of the weakest and shallowest in recent memory. I don’t see many good options for the Blue and Gold. You can expect many GMs to hand out major contracts to older players who arguably do not deserve lengthy, high-money deals, leading some teams to pursue blockbuster trades rather than overpaying free agents. That is where the Sabres probably fall.



If Buffalo does trade out a valuable but highly paid player like Greenway, the Sabres could use free agency to pick up a gritty, hard-hitting and willing to drop the gloves player for a savings. They especially might do this if the team passes on the physical Josh Dunne too, who is a UFA. If these two are gone next season, the Sabres would be very low on players who can handle themself when sh*t goes down. It doesn’t matter if you don’t like fighting in the game or not, every team needs at least one guy for the occasion.



Ross Johnston fits the bill for Buffalo. He is a 6’5″ 230 pound winger who played for the Ducks the last two seasons after many years with the Islanders. Johnston is a tough hombre’ who had four official fights last season, with many other skirmishes. The veteran is 32 years old. Another option is the monster that is Curtis Douglas. The 26 year old is 6’9″ and 242 pounds. He made his NHL debut last season, spliting time with the Canucks and goon-squad Tampa Bay Lightning. Douglas had a whopping 10 fights last season in only 43 games and would certainly be an intimidation factor. Neither of these gritty, tough guys would be regular players in the lineup, I imagine. But they would make solid, plug and play players used to face the right team though. Either bruiser could be had for the low-low price of $1.5 to $1.75 million per season.



Beyond some fill-in grit, I don’t see the Sabres making a big splash in free agency. In fact, I mostly hope they avoid it. The right big deal via a trade or two is the way to go, if Jarmo can swing it. And with that last thought, this b*tch of an article is now over. Those are your biggest areas of concern for Buffalo, a full plate for the team to handle this summer if they want a return visit to the playoffs. Commence the discussion.

stories.title: Buffalo Sabres Open Thread, Wk of 6/15
stories.summary:
The 2025-26 NHL season has come to an official end with the Carolina Hurricanes lifting Lord Stanley’s Cup after beating the Vegas Golden Knights 4-2 in the finals. 



This is going to be a mostly quiet week as the NHL gears up for the Entry Draft which is in a couple of weeks. 



Key dates



May 31 – June 6: NHL Scouting CombineJune 26 and 27: NHL DraftJune 29: Deadline for clubs to extend qualifying offers to RFAsJuly 1: Free agency begins

stories.title: 2025-26 Stanley Cup Final Open Thread
stories.summary:
The Buffalo Sabres return to the postseason didn’t go all the way to the Stanley Cup Finals like so many of us had hoped it would. 



Instead we have an expected and heavily favored Eastern Conference champions Carolina Hurricanes who have breezed through with a 12-1 record taking on a surprise quantity Vegas Golden Knights who got hot at the right time and then knocked out the overwhelming favorite and Presidents Trophy winners Colorado Avalanche in a sweep. 



Sabres fans have reason to dislike both teams and I won’t judge you for assuming hockey season is over. 



2026 Stanley Cup Final schedule



Game 1: Vegas 5, Carolina 4



Game 2: Carolina 4, Vegas, 3 (OT)



Game 3: Vegas 5, Carolina 4 (2OT)



Game 4: Carolina 5, Vegas, 3



Game 5: Carolina 4, Vegas, 2



Game 6: Carolina at Vegas, 8 p.m. ET, Sunday, June 14 (ABC, SN, CBC, TVAS)



*Game 7: Vegas at Carolina, 8 p.m. ET, Wednesday, June 17 (ABC, SN, CBC, TVAS)​



* – If necessary

stories.title: Anybody planning on going to the Bills game in Vegas this year?
stories.summary:
My group is starting to have the discussion.  Personally I am kinda torn if I even want to go.  I have a cousin that lives out there and he was telling me the Strip is just insanely overpriced now(Shocking I know...but he was saying they are really only catering to the elites now). I have been reading alot online about how Vegas has really gone downhill post Covid.  I've also cut back on drinking this past year. The stadium looks kinda cool I guess but it doesn't feel like a bucket list item to me.  Just not sure going out there for the game would be worth the $$$.



	 



	On the flipside I would hate to miss out on a great time, I've done the Nashville and New Orleans Bills trips and they were so much fun!  But this one just feels different to me I dunno



	 



	Anybody planning on going?
